Now we are pushing the accent into the future, which might just seem like a coincidence. But you can notice that it happens in other languages as well, this phenomenon. For example, in Greek part of the past tense is to pull the accent back one syllable, to pull the accent back into the past. And actually, this is so important in Greek that if there is not a syllable there to do it, you add one to pull the accent back into the past. So this is a phenomenon that you see in different languages. The accents representing somehow or reflecting somehow the concept of time and tenses. So here we have pushing the accent into the future: comeré. I will eat.
